name: Xbox 360 Wireless Controller
notes: Wireless receiver, not the controller itself. The receiver is the USB device; individual wireless controllers do not have USB PIDs. Real protocol uses vendor-specific USB class (0xFF/0x5D/0x01); nativeDescriptor=null reflects that there is no HID descriptor on the wire. PID 0x0291 is the v2 receiver. Descriptor mirrors xbox-360-type2's gamepad layout (X+Y, Rx+Ry, combined Z trigger, 10 buttons, hat) since wireless receivers typically pair with the standard gamepad subtype. Routes through hidmaestro_xusb.inf XUSB companion (matches xbox-360-wired path); no xinputhid driver mode (would force 16-button gamepad layout, breaking subtype fidelity).
